block_01: The US Bureau of labor statistics is set to release it’s monthly producer price index report, this week on Wednesday at 15:30 GMT+3
block_03: PPI measures the change of the price of goods and services on a manufacturing level. This means that PPI is a benchmark report for inflation levels on goods produced by corporations before they hit the shelves in retail stores.
block_04: Higher readings from forecasts tend to usually have a positive impact on the underlying currency.
block_06: PPI is one of the most important leading indicators of production activity. If producers charge higher prices on their finished goods to retailers, ultimately this higher price is passed on to consumers, leading to inflation.
block_07: Inflation data is the primary tool used by Central Banks in order to use monetary policies such as interest rates in order to control the growth rate of a country (inflation). High uncontrolled inflation could be devastating for an economy.
block_08: By assessing the outcome of the PPI report, investors try to take an early look on what the central bank could do with future monetary policies, usually positive reports could ultimately mean higher inflation and a hike on interest rates if all other factors remain equal. Higher interest rates tend to be positive for a sovereign currency. Investors try to adjust early figures with the future outlook of the central bank.
block_10: Latest growth report came in negative at 0% growth vs 0.2% expected growth. As a result, the Euro mildly appreciated against the Dollar for the first 15 minutes after the release of the report.
block_11: *Warning: Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future performance.
